svg.title: Enterprise LLM guardrails by layer, with a WAF box and Caddy tooling
svg.desc: LLM guardrails in three layers. Layer A perimeter includes a web application firewall box. Caddy appears in the tooling lines of the gateway-engine boxes. The takeaway summarises that Layer A decides if traffic flows, Layer B decides what passes, Layer C records it all.
svg.text: Enterprise LLM guardrails — by layer, open-source & self-hosted
Each control, the layer that enforces it, and open-source software you can run in Docker.
terminates TLS — makes Layer B possible. Your Guardrail labs.
mitmproxy (transparent) · Caddy / Envoy / Squid (explicit reverse proxy)
classic web-attack rules — SQLi, XSS — via the OWASP CRS ruleset.
Coraza + OWASP CRS · runs as a Caddy plugin or standalone
governs the content — needs the decrypted request
real key in gateway, not the app
serve repeats with no vendor call
scan the reply for unsafe or leaked content before the app sees it
Llama Guard · NeMo Guardrails · LLM Guard
check the reply is well-formed before the app consumes it
tokens & spend per app and team
A decides IF traffic flows · B decides WHAT passes · C records it all.
